issues.where: Step 1 — math helpers, mohas-roham.html line ~184 vs existing lines 2718-2720
issues.problem: easeInOut, easeOutCubic, easeOutBack ALREADY EXIST as `const` arrow functions at lines 2718-2720 (and are already used by the death-style switch at 2590/2606/2623 and the bow/nock UI at 2458-2486). The spec says to ADD all four near line 184. Re-declaring three `const` identifiers in the same module scope is a SyntaxError ('Identifier ... has already been declared') thrown at PARSE time — the whole single-file game fails to load (blank screen), not just the attack pass. This is the single highest-risk item in the spec and it is wrong about what exists. Only `remap` is genuinely missing (grep confirms zero matches).
issues.fix: Add ONLY `const remap=(u,a,b)=>clamp((u-a)/(b-a),0,1);` Place it next to clamp/lerp at line 184 (clamp is defined at 183, so no TDZ). Do NOT re-add easeInOut/easeOutCubic/easeOutBack. Either reuse the existing three at 2718-2720 as-is, or, if you want them available earlier for tidiness, MOVE (not duplicate) those three declarations up to ~184 and delete lines 2718-2720 — but moving is unnecessary since trollAI (2736+) and killMonster (2383+) both execute well after 2720 at runtime. Verify: page loads with no console SyntaxError before testing any attack.

issues.where: Step 4 (killMonster ~2384), Step 5 (endAtk), and every ATK fn that writes `torso` — all of trollAI
issues.problem: There is NO `m.torso`. `torso` is a local `const` inside makeMonster (line 1948) and is NEVER stored on the monster object — the `m={...}` literal at lines 2016-2022 exposes only g, legs, arms, head, eyes, eyeMat, bodySkin. So `m.torso.rotation.set(0,0,0)` in the killMonster patch and `m.torso`/bare `torso` in endAtk and in ATK.gruntSmash/bruteSlam/scoutClaw/shardJab/wispBite (all of which write torso.x/torso.z) will throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'rotation')` the first time ANY attack winds up or any troll is killed mid-swing. Bare `torso` inside trollAI is also a ReferenceError (makeMonster's const is not in scope). The death-style switch never touches torso, which is exactly WHY the spec wants to reset it — but the handle to do so does not exist.
issues.fix: In makeMonster, expose the torso group on the monster: add `torso,` to the `m={ g, legs, arms, head, ... }` literal at line 2016 (torso const is declared at 1948, before the literal, so it is in scope). Then use `m.torso` consistently in killMonster, endAtk, and pass torso into the ATK fns (e.g. `ATK[kind](m,u,dx,dz,d,lunge)` and have each fn write `m.torso.rotation.x`). Audit every `torso.` in the spec and prefix with `m.`. Verify: wind up a grunt and kill a brute mid-swing with no TypeError in console.

issues.where: Step 2 / VERIFY-ONLY — TROLL_EL presets at lines 1166-1169, consumed by elementSkin at line 1578
issues.problem: The spec's value-contrast pass and its VERIFY-ONLY gate both assume non-wisp element presets already keep emInt <= ~0.3. They do NOT: fire 0.55, ice 0.70, poison 0.55, lightning 0.60 — every one exceeds 0.3, ice by 2.3x. elementSkin writes these onto m.bodySkin for all non-wisp tinted trolls. High emissive on a flatShading material washes out the faceted shadow the whole silhouette rework relies on, so a fire/ice/poison/lightning-tinted troll reads as a featureless glowing blob and defeats the shape-based identity work (the load-bearing premise of the rework, per the random-tint constraint). The 'verify' as written would pass on a false assumption.
issues.fix: Treat this as a real CHANGE, not a verify. Clamp the four emInt values in TROLL_EL (lines 1166-1169) to ~0.25-0.30 before/with the value-contrast pass (e.g. fire 0.30, ice 0.30, poison 0.28, lightning 0.30). Re-check the lit look afterward — the aura sprite (line 1581-1582) already supplies the colored glow, so the body emissive can drop without losing the elemental read. Verify in-engine, not by code inspection: spawn an ice-tinted grunt and a fire-tinted grunt and confirm facet shadows still define the body.

issues.where: lunge() vs separateMonsters() — trollAI lunge writes p each frame; separateMonsters at line 2671/2724-2735 runs every frame after the AI loop
issues.problem: separateMonsters writes g.position.x/z directly to resolve troll-vs-troll overlap. lunge() re-derives p.x/p.z from a frozen lungeBase every frame (by design, so it doesn't compound). Net: for the full duration of any swing (~0.3s shard up to ~1.5s brute) the lunging troll discards every separation push the previous frame applied — separation is effectively disabled mid-swing, so attacking trolls can stack/interpenetrate, and with up to 14 trolls several may swing at once. Not a crash, but it visibly undoes the anti-crowding system precisely when many trolls cluster on the player. lunge also bypasses pushMob (line 2750), so a lunge can shove a troll into a tree/rock collider for the swing.
issues.fix: Either (a) accept it and keep lunge amounts small (the spec's 0.15-0.45 are modest, so overlap is minor) and note it as expected, or (b) update lungeBase by the separation delta: after separateMonsters, fold any positional correction back into lungeBase, or have lunge add its offset to the CURRENT p rather than a frozen base on frames where separation moved it. Simplest low-risk option: leave as-is but clamp lunge contribution and confirm visually that mid-swing stacking is acceptable. Also run the lunged p through pushMob so a lunge can't push a troll through a solid.

issues.where: Step 5 co-op hardening claim — trollAI is never called for remote trolls (lines 2663-2664)
issues.problem: The spec says to harden 'any coopIsRemote return' inside the attack clock so remote trolls only walk and can't freeze in a half-lunge. But remote trolls SKIP trollAI entirely (line 2663: `if(coopIsRemote(m)){ /*skip*/ } else trollAI(...)`), and their transforms are interpolated in coopTick (line 3084+). So no atkPhase/lunge code ever runs for a remote troll — there is nothing to freeze and the only coopIsRemote return inside trollAI is the shaman one (line 2776), which is already a plain `break`. The hardening is mostly a no-op as described; effort there is wasted and may give false confidence.
issues.fix: Drop the 'remote trolls only walk' hardening as unnecessary — instead just add an assertion/comment that remote trolls never enter trollAI. DO still harden the genuinely reachable early-return: the rooted/frozenSolid block at line 2745 (`return` mid-swing leaves atkPhase>0 and lungeBase set). Add there: if(m.lungeBase){ p.x=m.lungeBase.x; p.z=m.lungeBase.z; m.lungeBase=null; } m.atkPhase=0; m.atkKind=null; before the existing return, so a rooted/frozen troll snaps to guard with no positional drift. Verify: root a troll mid-swing (lightning stun) — no frozen half-lunge.

issues.where: PERF — Step 3 shared-pass split + per-type adds, vs warmup at lines 2824-2851 and 14-troll cap (line 2675)
issues.problem: Mesh budget is genuinely roughly flat and the worth-it/noise judgment in the spec is sound, with caveats. embellishTroll drops ~16->9 shared meshes for the 6 non-wisp types (real win, multiplied across up to 14 live trolls + brute splits). The per-type adds are mostly cheap low-poly primitives (Dodeca/Cone/Sphere at 4-9 segments). Two cost notes the spec under-weights: (1) WISP gains ~5 NEW meshes INCLUDING opaque non-additive cloth — wisps were previously near-free (transparent sprites); making them solid hooded wraiths adds real draw + shadow cost, and ROOT CAUSE 6 raises wisp spawn share at wave2 from ~5.5% to ~14%, so more of them on screen at once. (2) warmup (line 2826) already builds one of EACH of the 7 types, so every new mesh/material is pre-rendered once — good for hitch-free first encounter, but the wisp cloth material is a new opaque std material that must compile; ensure it's a SHARED const (not per-monster) or warmup won't actually pre-compile the per-instance one. The brute +2 lit pauldron caps and shard +2 plates are noise-level and worth it for silhouette.
issues.fix: Keep the shared-pass diet (clear win). For wisp: make the new `cloth` material a single shared module-level const (like lichenMat at 1546), NOT created inside build() per monster, so (a) warmup pre-compiles it and (b) you don't leak N materials — and confirm disposeMonster (2034-2039) won't try to dispose a shared material. Given wisp also casts shadow now, double-check the shadow-mesh whitelist at line 2029 (only torso/head/legs/arms cast) still covers the wisp's new mantle/face-plate parented to g/torso, or wisps won't shadow-cast (probably fine/desirable for perf). Hold the line on the 'no extra accents' budget note.

issues.where: Wisp fix (gameplay) — TROLL_TYPES.wisp line 1590, build line 1592-1599, embellishTroll early-return line 1548
issues.problem: The wisp gameplay fixes are coherent and correctly target the real reasons it's never seen (one-shot fragility via hpMul 0.6 + fire resist 1.5; too fast; reads as a particle). Risks to the 'fast fragile spirit / ice-counter' role: hpMul 0.6->1.0 plus fire resist 1.5->1.0 is a DOUBLE survivability buff (more HP AND no fire penalty) stacked with speedMul 1.55->1.2 and speedCap 6->4.5 (slower) and scaleMul 0.7->0.95 (bigger). That is a large simultaneous swing — the wisp could flip from never-seen to a tanky, easy-to-hit chore that no longer feels like a darting spirit, and the ice-counter identity weakens if it's now a slow easy target you don't need ice for. The visual rework itself (opacity 0.6->0.92, opaque mantle, cut additive core) is the safe, load-bearing part and is well-reasoned; bodySkin is a per-monster clone (line 1594 / dispose at 2037) so the opacity bump is safe.
issues.fix: Change one survivability axis at a time and playtest between. Start with the visual rework + speed/scale (so it lingers and reads) and ONLY the fire-resist 1.5->1.0 fix; leave hpMul at 0.6 or bump to ~0.8 first. Re-evaluate before also taking hpMul to 1.0 — surviving one ~48 charged arrow is the stated goal, and 0.8 (wave5 ~51 HP) may already clear that without making it a tank. Keep ice 0.35. Verify in-engine that it still feels like a quick spirit, not a walking target, and that ice still trivializes it (ROOT CAUSE theme intact).

goAheadGuards: Implement ONLY `const remap` near line 184. Do NOT add easeInOut/easeOutCubic/easeOutBack — they already exist at lines 2718-2720; re-declaring them is a parse-time SyntaxError that blanks the whole game. Confirm page loads clean before anything else.

Add `torso,` to the m={...} literal at line 2016 FIRST, then make every torso write in killMonster/endAtk/ATK fns use `m.torso` (bare `torso` is out of scope in trollAI; `m.torso` is currently undefined). Without this, every windup and every mid-swing kill throws a TypeError.

Clamp TROLL_EL emInt (lines 1166-1169: fire .55/ice .70/poison .55/lightning .60) down to ~0.25-0.30 as an actual change — the spec's 'verify emInt <= 0.3' is false on current code, and high emissive on flatShading erases the silhouette the rework depends on.

Make the new wisp `cloth` material a single shared module-level const (not per-build()), so warmup (line 2826) pre-compiles it and disposeMonster (2034-2039) doesn't dispose a shared material; verify wisp shadow-cast whitelist at line 2029 covers (or intentionally excludes) the new mantle/face meshes.

Stage the wisp survivability buffs: do fire-resist 1.5->1.0 + speed/scale + visual first; hold hpMul at 0.6-0.8 and playtest before taking it to 1.0, to avoid flipping the wisp from never-seen to a slow tank and to preserve the ice-counter identity.

Skip the 'remote co-op trolls only walk' hardening (remote trolls never enter trollAI, line 2663 — it's a no-op). DO harden the reachable rooted/frozenSolid early-return at line 2745 to clear atkPhase/lungeBase so a paralyzed troll can't freeze in a half-lunge. Co-op is otherwise safe: damagePlayer (2364) only touches the local player, so deferring onHit to the strike sub-frame changes nothing on the wire.

Accept that lunge() (re-deriving p from a frozen lungeBase each frame) nullifies separateMonsters pushes for the swing duration — keep lunge amounts small (the spec's 0.15-0.45 are fine) and run the lunged position through pushMob (line 2750 pattern) so a lunge can't clip a troll into a tree/rock.
